commands.review_summary:
The PR removes the raw-mode save guard for snapshots without original raw text and adds a controller test asserting that `saveConfig` submits generated raw text with the snapshot hash.

PR surface: Source -3, Tests +23. Total +20 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence real user reproduction path is shown. The PR demonstrates a synthetic controller state where raw mode is forced while `snapshot.raw` is null, but current UI and docs intentionally prevent that state from acting as raw mode.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer decision on whether to change the raw-unavailable and SecretRef round-trip contract; this is not a safe automated repair because the central diff is the contested behavior.

target.title: fix(control-ui): save generated raw config text
target.body:
## What changed

- Removes the save-time rejection for raw mode when the current snapshot did not include original raw text.
- Adds a controller test proving `saveConfig` submits the already-generated raw config text with the snapshot base hash.

## Why

`applyConfigSnapshot` can populate `configRaw` by serializing editable structured config when `snapshot.raw` is absent. Once that generated raw text exists, the save path should submit it like any other raw draft.

The previous guard checked only whether the snapshot originally contained raw text. That rejected a valid generated raw draft even though `configRaw` already held the text that should be saved.

## Validation

- `pnpm --dir ui test src/ui/controllers/config.test.ts`
